description: Downtown Toronto baseline. Morning commute, normal conditions.
Highway 401 eastbound near Weston Rd. Congestion building. All lanes slow — likely volume-driven.
Vehicle running red light at Eglinton Ave and Dufferin St northbound. Speed estimated 70 km/h in 50 zone.
Cyclist running stop sign on Roncesvalles Ave heading south. Near-miss with pedestrian.
Two-vehicle collision at Yonge St and Davisville Ave. Vehicles blocking curb lane. Airbags deployed on one vehicle. No fire.
Ambulance and police on scene at Yonge and Davisville crash. Paramedics attending to driver.
Person sprinting east on King St near St. Lawrence Market. No visible pursuer. Possible late for transit.
Physical altercation between two individuals on Queen St West near Spadina. Third person attempting to intervene. No weapons visible.
Queen and Spadina — police dispersed the altercation. Scene clear.
Allen Expressway southbound backed up to Lawrence Ave. Estimated 25-minute delay. School zone hours.
Downtown Toronto. Afternoon settling in. No active incidents. System quiet.
Downtown Toronto baseline. Overcast skies, light rain beginning.
Lower Don Trail underpass at Eastern Ave — flooded. Road impassable, 40 cm standing water.
TFRS water rescue boats deployed on flooded Don Valley Pkwy southbound. Two vehicles stranded in approximately 1 metre of water.
DVP southbound fully closed at Richmond St E. Significant standing water, abandoned vehicles visible.
Paramedics responding to stranded resident in flooded basement — Rosedale Ave near Bloor.
Humber River overflowing banks near Old Mill. Riverside Drive flooded for 600 metres. Evacuation order for adjacent streets.
Multiple residents evacuating on foot along Roncesvalles Ave heading north. Some carrying bags. Panic visible.
Lake Shore Blvd West near Bathurst — major inundation. Several vehicles partially submerged. Waterline at traffic light bases.
Don Valley rescue winding down. Last stranded vehicle reached. 7 civilians rescued. 2 transported to hospital — hypothermia, non-life-threatening.
Flood event subsiding. DVP remains closed. Emergency services still active. Recovery phase beginning.
Toronto baseline. Morning calm. No anomalies.
Large protest gathering forming at Nathan Phillips Square. Estimated 3,000 people. Crowd agitated, pushing against police lines. Signs in multiple languages.
Simultaneous protest at Parliament Hill Ottawa. Crowd blocking all vehicle access to Wellington St. Police forming defensive line.
Protest march on Georgia St Vancouver heading toward Vancouver Art Gallery. 2,000 participants. Traffic blocked in both directions.
Ottawa — vehicles set on fire on Wellington St. Smoke column visible from Parliament. Police requesting reinforcements. Government district access severed.
Toronto riot spreading from City Hall toward financial district. Looters visible on Bay St. Police lines broken. Ambulances unable to reach injured.
Vancouver — riot police deployed. Water cannon activated. Crowd fragmenting into groups moving toward port and Burrard Bridge.
Toronto financial district — civilians fleeing the riot moving south toward Lake Shore. Dozens of people running in the same direction.
Vancouver — crowd reached port access roads on Burrard Inlet. Port security gates breached. Cargo operations halted.
Toronto east of riot zone — streets emptying. Businesses closing early. Transit disrupted. City going quiet.
Ottawa government district locked down. Streets around Parliament empty. Armed RCMP units visible. No resolution in sight.
All systems nominal. Toronto baseline. No anomalies detected.
Ottawa security camera: delta-wing silhouettes at ~150 m altitude. 8–12 units heading southwest toward Toronto.
CFB Valcartier perimeter camera: impact on vehicle depot. Fire visible across multiple buildings.
Ottawa traffic camera: Shahed formation overflying Rideau Canal at ~180 km/h heading 225° toward Toronto.
Coast Guard visual contact, open Pacific west of Haida Gwaii: drone breaking from a fishing-vessel-disguised launch platform, heading toward Prince Rupert.
Coast Guard visual contact, open Pacific west of Haida Gwaii: drone breaking from a fishing-vessel-disguised launch platform, heading toward Prince Rupert.
Coast Guard visual contact, open Pacific west of Haida Gwaii: drone breaking from a fishing-vessel-disguised launch platform, heading toward Prince Rupert.
Halifax Stanfield Airport camera: runway impact. Terminal evacuated, all flights grounded.
Coast Guard visual contact, open Pacific west of Haida Gwaii: drone breaking from a fishing-vessel-disguised launch platform, heading toward Prince Rupert.
Coast Guard visual contact, open Pacific west of Haida Gwaii: drone breaking from a fishing-vessel-disguised launch platform, heading toward Prince Rupert.
CFB Comox perimeter camera: impact on flight line. Search-and-rescue squadron hangar burning.
Coast Guard visual contact, open Pacific west of Haida Gwaii: drone breaking from a fishing-vessel-disguised launch platform, heading toward Prince Rupert.
Coast Guard visual contact, open Pacific west of Haida Gwaii: drone breaking from a fishing-vessel-disguised launch platform, heading toward Prince Rupert.
Coast Guard visual contact, open Pacific west of Haida Gwaii: drone breaking from a fishing-vessel-disguised launch platform, heading toward Prince Rupert.
Coast Guard visual contact, open Pacific west of Haida Gwaii: drone breaking from a fishing-vessel-disguised launch platform, heading toward Prince Rupert.
Explosion at the Halifax transatlantic cable landing station — international connectivity degraded, not just local.
Coast Guard visual contact, open Pacific west of Haida Gwaii: drone breaking from a fishing-vessel-disguised launch platform, heading toward Prince Rupert.
Explosion at the Bamfield transpacific cable landing station — paired with the Halifax strike, both coasts' international links targeted together.
Trudeau Airport camera: transport helicopters discharging troops onto the runway.
Coast Guard visual contact, open Pacific west of Haida Gwaii: drone breaking from a fishing-vessel-disguised launch platform, heading toward Prince Rupert.
Coast Guard visual contact, open Pacific west of Haida Gwaii: drone breaking from a fishing-vessel-disguised launch platform, heading toward Prince Rupert.
Highway camera near Regina: delta-wing silhouettes low and fast, heading in three directions.
Pearson Airport perimeter camera: impact on runway 06L. Blast crater visible. Radar tower destroyed. All aircraft grounded. Fire suppression active.
Trudeau Airport perimeter camera: terminal secured, troops moving to control tower.
Calgary International Airport camera: impact near the terminal, telecom exchange building adjacent also struck.
Highway 401 camera near Trenton: delta-wing silhouettes descending toward the base perimeter.
CFB Edmonton Garrison: impact on vehicle motor pool. Fires spreading between buildings.
Halifax: evacuation order in effect. Streets emptying, vehicles backed up on evacuation routes out of the city.
Winnipeg: evacuation order in effect. Streets emptying, vehicles backed up on evacuation routes out of the city.
CFB Petawawa perimeter camera: impact on garrison motor pool. Army's largest eastern base now under direct attack.
Calgary: evacuation order in effect. Streets emptying, vehicles backed up on evacuation routes out of the city.
Camera near Hamilton: Ka-52 attack helicopter formation moving west ahead of ground column. Low altitude, combat configuration.
Drone recon over Halifax approaches: landing craft visible, armor being unloaded on the waterfront.
Montreal: evacuation order in effect. Streets emptying, vehicles backed up on evacuation routes out of the city.
Drone recon over Esquimalt: landing craft at the dockyard, armor disembarking under cover of the earlier strike.
Winnipeg airport camera: transport helicopters discharging troops onto the runway.
Victoria: evacuation order in effect. Streets emptying, vehicles backed up on evacuation routes out of the city.
Fredericton: Civilians fleeing on foot along the Saint John River pathway, sirens audible across the river.
Halifax waterfront camera: first tanks ashore, forming up. Troops moving toward Bedford Highway.
Calgary airport camera: transport helicopters discharging troops onto the runway.
Esquimalt dockyard camera: first tanks ashore, forming up near the naval base perimeter.
Nanaimo: evacuation order in effect. Streets emptying, vehicles backed up on evacuation routes out of the city.
Regina: Downtown streets empty, businesses shuttered, distant smoke visible to the east.
Moncton: evacuation order in effect. Streets emptying, vehicles backed up on evacuation routes out of the city.
Kelowna: Highway 97 gridlocked, drivers abandoning vehicles to flee on foot toward the lake.

Pearson Airport perimeter camera: military transport helicopters discharging troops directly onto runway and taxiways. Assault teams moving toward terminal buildings. Armed resistance from airport security overrun within minutes.
Winnipeg airport perimeter camera: terminal secured, troops moving to control tower.
Iqaluit: evacuation order in effect. Streets emptying, vehicles backed up on evacuation routes out of the city.
Calgary airport perimeter camera: terminal secured, troops moving to control tower.
Thunder Bay: Crowds moving away from the harbourfront after reports of aircraft overhead.
Highway 102 camera: lead tanks moving north at speed. Drone formation flying escort overhead.
Malahat summit camera: lead tanks climbing the grade. Drone formation flying escort overhead.
Saskatoon: University area evacuated, streets empty except for emergency vehicles.
Charlottetown: Waterfront evacuated after reports of naval activity in the strait.
YVR Sea Island perimeter camera: transport helicopters landing on runway 08R. Troops deploying rapidly. Control tower under assault. YVR radar already dark from earlier Shahed strike.
Prince George: Downtown core empty following province-wide emergency broadcast.
Sudbury: Highway 17 backed up for kilometres, civilians heading north away from the corridor.
Truro town camera: lead tanks at the rail junction. Drone formation still overhead.
Nanaimo camera: lead tanks near the BC Ferries terminal. Drone formation still overhead.
Highway 2 near Moncton: column visible at range, drone formation overhead providing recon.
Highway 19 near Parksville: column visible at range, drone formation overhead providing recon.
Downtown Moncton — lead tank at the Petitcodiac River bridge. Urban combat beginning.
Windsor camera Riverside Drive East: T-90 tanks and BTR-82A APCs on Canadian soil. Ka-52 helicopter visible above the column.
Downtown Parksville — lead tank at the town centre. Urban combat beginning.
Greater Moncton Airport camera: troops from transport helicopters seizing the terminal, same pattern as Pearson and YVR.
Qualicum Beach airstrip camera: troops from transport helicopters seizing the runway, same pattern as Pearson and YVR.
BC camera Pacific Highway: armored column entering Canada. Attack helicopters providing top cover.
Toronto: small recon-pattern drone re-observing the earlier strike site, likely battle damage assessment.
Highway 401 east of Windsor: lead tanks at 55 km/h. Column 6 km. Ka-52s engaging resistance positions ahead.
Vancouver: small recon-pattern drone re-observing the earlier strike site, likely battle damage assessment.
Halifax: small recon-pattern drone re-observing the earlier strike site, likely battle damage assessment.
Winnipeg: small recon-pattern drone re-observing the earlier strike site, likely battle damage assessment.
Trans-Canada near Abbotsford: 35+ armored vehicles. Helicopter support overhead. Secondary force splitting onto Highway 9.
London Ontario 401: lead T-90 at Wonderland Rd. Ka-52 helicopter strafing ahead. OPP roadblock overrun.
London street cameras: mass civilian evacuation on foot on Richmond St North. Hundreds fleeing. Vehicles abandoned.
BC — Lougheed Highway Coquitlam: lead tank approaching Port Coquitlam. Helicopters over Burnaby Mountain engaging defenders.
Highway 401 Mississauga Credit River: tanks inside GTA. Ka-52s ranging over Etobicoke.
Downtown Toronto — evacuation order in effect. Subway shut down. Streets empty. Helicopter rotor noise audible to the west.
BC — Burnaby Kingsway: tanks rolling west. Attack helicopters providing close air support. Fire at Metrotown.
Highway 401 at Keele Toronto: lead tanks inside city limits. Ka-52 helicopters over downtown.
Downtown Toronto — civilian resistance on King St West. Small arms fire from buildings. Helicopters firing rockets at building positions. Urban combat.
Vancouver — Burrard Street camera: lead tank at bridge north end entering downtown. Desant troops landing from helicopters at False Creek simultaneously.
